message:
feat(scheduler): integrate I/O driver into three-lane worker loop

Workers now participate in a leader/follower pattern for reactor polling,
ensuring I/O readiness events (e.g. TCP connect completion) are processed
promptly even when no tasks are in flight.

Changes:
- Acquire IoDriverHandle alongside TimerDriverHandle at scheduler init
- Store io_driver on ThreeLaneWorker
- Add new Phase 5 (I/O driving) between task stealing and backoff:
  workers try_lock the IO driver and call turn_with(1ms) to poll
- Adjust parking logic: when IO driver is present but no timer deadline,
  use short 1ms park timeout instead of parking indefinitely, so workers
  periodically poll the reactor
- Add trace!() calls to inject_ready for debugging task scheduling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
